sourceMovie.title: Shaft
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Shaft works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 16 Blocks
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Shaft clicked for you, 16 Blocks (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Equalizer 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Shaft clicked for you, The Equalizer 3 (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Shaft?
faq.answer: Try 16 Blocks, The Equalizer 3, Death Wish—each shares DNA with Shaft while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Shaft?
faq.answer: Shaft (2000) is best described as Action, Adventure, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Shaft part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Shaft (Reboot) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
